schema_notes: Reference implementation for per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual reference run — single-subject deep research (Buffett = top-giving anchor / ceiling reference) + regen_v3@sha256-e85194901647731c + regen_v3@sha256-02ea543480d7485a + regen_v3@sha256-69d8f07a859faae6

person.wealth_source: Berkshire Hathaway — chairman, CEO (stepping down end of 2025) and largest individual shareholder; essentially 100% Berkshire common stock at time of 2006 pledge (98%+ of net worth)

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 profile lists Buffett at #9 on the Billionaires list. Wikipedia cites $148.9B as of Jan 2026; TheStreet cites $146.5B as of Jan 7, 2026. The narrow spread reflects Berkshire's public, liquid common stock — Buffett's net worth is among the most observable on the Forbes list precisely because 98%+ is/was in one public security tracked daily.

sources.source_note: Forbes profile page; exact value not retrieved due to Forbes block — cross-corroborated via Wikipedia citing Forbes Jan 2026.

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Buffett's wealth is ~98% Berkshire Hathaway public common stock (Class A convertible 1:1500 to Class B). Unlike Ellison (founder-CEO of Oracle with 41% stake), Buffett's Berkshire ownership is under 20% and Berkshire has deep daily trading volume in Class B. Apply 85% liquidity — only haircut is the signaling effect of large block sales, not structural lock-up. For pledge-fulfillment denominator purposes, treat essentially all of it as disposable. This is why Buffett is the ceiling reference: his wealth is maximally legible and maximally deployable.

cited_events.recipient: New charitable trust to be run jointly by Howard, Susan, and Peter Buffett
cited_events.notes: November 2024 will update: 99.5% of remaining wealth at death to a new charitable trust overseen by his three children. Gates Foundation donations to cease at death. This is a MATERIAL amendment to the original 2006 commitment (which directed ~83% to Gates) and redirects the post-lifetime giving to family-controlled vehicles.
cited_events.methodology_note: Under IPS methodology, transfers to family-controlled foundations/trusts are not fully counted as 'out-the-door' because the family retains grantmaking control. Under Forbes/Chronicle, transfers to any 501(c)(3) count. FLAG for both framings.

cited_events.notes: Nov 2025 Fortune: Buffett admits his original Giving Pledge timeline was 'not feasible' — left his three kids $500M each per year to give away ($1.5B/year collective). Novel structure: children act as pass-through grantmakers during his lifetime. This is additive to foundation gifts — each child gets $500M/yr of discretionary giving authority.

Buffett resigned as Gates Foundation trustee June 2021 — giving continued but governance role ended. In April 2026 reporting (CNBC) Buffett signaled possible end to Gates donations entirely citing Bill Gates's Epstein ties; already formalized in Nov 2024 will update.

pledged_recipients: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation (primary, ~5x share of family foundations combined)
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Original commitment June 2006: gift 85% of Berkshire stock via annual installments to five foundations. Mechanism: convert Class A to Class B, then annual gifts of B shares decreasing by 5%/yr from initial pledged share counts (i.e. each year's gift = 0.95^n × initial annual amount, so infinite-series total ≈ 20× first-year gift). Gates Foundation initially received the largest share.

pledges_and_announcements.status_source: IPS Giving Pledge at 15 (July 2025) — Buffett is a co-founder of the Giving Pledge. IPS report identifies only 9 of 256 signers as having fulfilled, with Laura and John Arnold as the sole living 2010 signers categorized as fully fulfilled. Buffett's classification in IPS terms is NOT 'fulfilled' under IPS's strict gave-away-half-of-current-wealth metric because his net worth has grown faster than his giving. However, under Forbes/Chronicle methodology (cumulative giving exceeding 50% of baseline-at-pledge wealth), Buffett's $60B+ out-the-door vs $43B at 2006 commitment is ~140% of original baseline — fulfilled by that measure.
pledges_and_announcements.pledge_letter_quote: More than 99% of my wealth will go to philanthropy during my lifetime or at death. Measured by dollars, this commitment is large. In a comparison with most families, however, it requires no sacrifice. ... The reaction of my family and me to our extraordinary good fortune is not guilt, but rather gratitude.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Co-founder (with Bill Gates and Melinda French Gates). Wealth at 2010 signing ~$47B. 99% target in 2010 dollars = ~$46.5B. Current net worth ~$149B; 99% target now = ~$147.5B. Lifetime giving $60B+ exceeds 2010 pledge baseline but is ~40% of current-NW target — tension because net worth compounded faster than giving.
pledges_and_announcements.methodology_note_for_dedup: Two legitimate framings exist. (A) BASELINE-FIXED: pledger committed to give half of wealth-at-signing; $60B giving vs $47B signing NW = fulfilled. (B) FLOATING: pledger committed to give half of wealth-at-death; current gap is ($147B - $60B) = $87B to go with finite remaining time. Forbes/Chronicle use (A); IPS uses (B). Publish both framings side-by-side for Buffett.

pledges_and_announcements.extraction_note: Warren Buffett pledged approximately $3 billion in Berkshire-Hathaway stock to the Susan Thompson Buffett Foundation in 2006 to be paid out over time.
pledges_and_announcements.extraction_evidence: In 2006, Warren Buffett pledged almost $3 billion worth of his Berkshire-Hathaway, Inc. stock to the foundation

pledges_and_announcements.extraction_note: Warren Buffett announced that his $144 billion fortune would be transferred to a charitable trust overseen by his three children.
pledges_and_announcements.extraction_evidence: his $144 billion fortune would be transferred to a charitable trust that his three children would oversee

pledges_and_announcements.extraction_note: Warren Buffett pledged $2.5 million to the Nuclear Threat Initiative to help reduce nuclear and biological threats.
pledges_and_announcements.extraction_evidence: Warren Buffett Pledges $2.5 Million to Nuclear Threat Initiative
pledges_and_announcements.regen_query: "Warren Buffett" pledges OR commits OR announces

pledges_and_announcements.extraction_evidence: Warren Buffett Pledges $50 Million to Develop International Nuclear Fuel Bank
pledges_and_announcements.regen_query: "Warren Buffett" pledges OR commits OR announces

pledges_and_announcements.extraction_note: Warren Buffett pledged 99% of his wealth to philanthropy for global health, education, and poverty relief.
pledges_and_announcements.extraction_evidence: Warren Buffett pledges 99% of his wealth to philanthropy, funding global health, education, and poverty relief
pledges_and_announcements.regen_query: "Warren Buffett" pledges OR commits OR announces

pledges_and_announcements.extraction_note: Warren Buffett pledges $100 billion to a yet-to-be-named charitable trust, though the specific charitable purpose is not detailed in the snippet.
pledges_and_announcements.extraction_evidence: Warren Buffett's yet-to-be-named charitable trust will be 100% effective from day one
pledges_and_announcements.regen_query: "Warren Buffett" pledges OR commits OR announces

pledges_and_announcements.extraction_note: Warren Buffett announced a donation of $3.6 billion to charity, with cumulative gifts totaling $34 billion since 2006.
pledges_and_announcements.extraction_evidence: Warren Buffett is donating $3.6 billion more to charity
pledges_and_announcements.regen_query: "Warren Buffett" 2018 OR 2019 OR 2020 gift OR donation revealed OR uncovered

political_giving.substitution_note: Not relevant — Buffett is historically one of the lowest-political-giving billionaires in the Forbes top 10. NBER W26616 substitution effect (political displaces charitable at $0.33/$1) does not apply meaningfully here.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Approximate lifetime political giving. NBC (2007) reported $65,600 in federal candidate giving 1992-2007. CNN Money (2015) reported $329,000 in political donations since 2000. Yahoo/AFP (2024): last reported political donation Nov 2019 ($106,500 to DCCC). Buffett has publicly stated he will not give millions to candidates; he is an outspoken critic of the role of money in politics. Total lifetime < $1M — approximately 0.002% of charitable giving.

sources.notes: AP fact-check: Buffett did NOT donate to Biden's 2020 campaign; Bosanek (Buffett's assistant) confirmed zero giving to Biden.

political_giving.note: Buffett is the cleanest political-giving datapoint in the Forbes top 10. Useful as a control variable: political giving cannot explain the gap between Buffett's charitable giving and other top-10 billionaires' giving, because Buffett's political floor is essentially zero.

foundations_receiving.name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation (Trust)
foundations_receiving.note: ~72% of Buffett's cumulative giving through 2025. Terminates at Buffett's death per Nov 2024 will update.

foundations_receiving.name: The Susan Thompson Buffett Foundation
foundations_receiving.cumulative_buffett_contribution_usd_note: Named for Buffett's late first wife (d. 2004). Receives annual ~5-8% of the family-foundation slice. Part of the ~$17B cumulative family-foundation giving through 2025.

foundations_receiving.name: Sherwood Foundation
foundations_receiving.cumulative_buffett_contribution_usd_note: Receives equal slice with Howard and NoVo among daughter/sons' foundations. Was #10 among top foundations by 2017-2019 disbursements ($568M).

foundations_receiving.name: The Howard G. Buffett Foundation
foundations_receiving.focus: Food security, conflict mitigation, human trafficking, Illinois/Nebraska/Arizona/Texas public safety
foundations_receiving.cumulative_buffett_contribution_usd_note: Receives equal annual slice with Sherwood and NoVo.

foundations_receiving.name: NoVo Foundation
foundations_receiving.focus: Women and girls, Upstate NY place-based giving
foundations_receiving.cumulative_buffett_contribution_usd_note: Initial Warren contribution: 350,000 Berkshire A-shares, valued at ~$1B. Receives equal annual slice with Sherwood and Howard G. Buffett Foundation.

foundations_receiving.name: Letters Foundation + Learning By Giving Foundation
foundations_receiving.notes: Smaller recipients of Buffett support; not part of the main five-foundation annual transfer schedule.

detected_vehicles.foundation_own_note: CRITICAL CONCEPTUAL POINT: Buffett does NOT operate his own named foundation. He is the FUNDING SOURCE for five external foundations via annual Berkshire share transfers. This is structurally different from Ellison (LEF = Ellison's own 501(c)(3)) or Gates (BMGF was his own foundation while he chaired it). Buffett's model is: transfer stock to pre-existing recipient foundations, which then make their own grants out. For the Scrooge-score 'observable_giving' measure, we count Buffett's out-the-door transfers (the $60B cumulative), not the recipient foundations' subsequent grants-out (which would be the correct measure for Bill Gates but NOT for Buffett — counting both would double-count).
detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No DAF activity identified. Buffett gives directly to named 501(c)(3) foundations with Form 4 disclosure. Near-zero DAF uplift expected.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: None. Buffett famously disclaims the LLC model.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: Not checked (Phase 2) but extremely unlikely — Buffett is an outspoken advocate of higher US taxes on the wealthy and has no known offshore structuring.

trusts_disclosed.classification_note: Explicitly charitable — 99.5% of remaining wealth at death. Unlike Ellison's opaque 'trust of unknown structure', Buffett's trust is publicly committed to charitable disposition. But governance is family-controlled, which IPS would flag as 'dynastic philanthropy' even though Forbes/Chronicle would count it.

red_flags_not_present: GIVING_PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED — Under baseline-fixed methodology, Buffett's $60B+ giving exceeds the $47B 2010 pledge baseline. Under floating methodology he remains on track given the announced $1.5B/yr children's pass-through and 99.5% estate trust.
POLITICAL_DOMINATES_CHARITABLE — Political giving is <0.002% of charitable giving. Opposite of this flag.
FOR_PROFIT_PIVOT — No for-profit philanthropic pivot. All giving is to 501(c)(3)s.
LOW_PAYOUT — Not applicable: Buffett is the source, not the foundation. Recipient foundations (Gates, STBF, Sherwood, etc.) all have payout rates above 5%.
HIGH_OFFICER_COMP — Not applicable: Buffett draws no compensation from philanthropic entities.

NET_WORTH_SURGE_WITHOUT_GIVING_MATCH — Net worth has grown substantially since 2006 (~$43B → $149B, ~3.5x), but giving has grown in parallel ($0 → $60B out-the-door). Under cumulative-out vs NW-at-signing, giving exceeds baseline by ~40%. This is the OPPOSITE of the Ellison pattern.

rollup.observable_giving_method: Anchor on CNBC July 2025 cumulative figure ("over $60 billion since 2006"). Do NOT sum itemized annual events — they are already inside this anchor. Subject to SEC Form 4 G-transaction verification (Phase 1.2) which should corroborate exactly.
observable_giving_todo: Pull SEC Form 4 G-transactions for Buffett's CIK 315090 2006-2025 — should reconcile to $60B exactly.
Pull Gates Foundation Trust 990-PF contributions-received to cross-check the $43B cumulative-to-Gates anchor.
Itemize 2011-2022 annual gifts to replace the roll-up placeholder event with year-by-year events (SEC Form 4 parser does this mechanically).
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.85 × $148.9B = $126.6B; tenure cap saturated (40 years). Expected = $12.66B. This is the STRICT 10% formal norm applied to current wealth.

hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not checked. Zero default — near-zero expected given Buffett's pro-tax public stance.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_note: Buffett explicitly eschews anonymous giving — his entire philanthropic method is public, SEC-disclosed share transfers to named foundations. Zero cohort uplift for him specifically.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Buffett is publicly agnostic/secular. No tithing norm applies. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.estate_commitment_method: Nov 2024 will: 99.5% of remaining wealth at death to new charitable trust. At current $148.9B net worth minus ~$1B personal retention = ~$147B committed at death. Unlike Ellison's 'unknown trust structure', this is publicly committed to charity — but trustees are family members so IPS would flag as dynastic. Include in hidden_upper as a distinct category (not 'hidden' in the opacity sense, but 'committed but not yet disbursed').
hidden_upper_usd.children_passthrough_note: Nov 2025 announcement: $500M/yr × 3 children = $1.5B/yr of additional discretionary giving routed through children. Annual, not cumulative. Included here as forward-looking channel; not yet disbursed.
hidden_upper_usd.total_method: estate_commitment ($147B) + children_passthrough ($1.5B annual-next-year) — everything else zero for Buffett

rollup.tier_rule: Buffett does not fit the Tier A/B/C (scrooge-flagged) schema. He is the positive-reference ceiling. Observable giving $60B is 4.74× the strict 10% norm; cumulative out-the-door + committed-at-death is ~$207B against $148.9B current NW (meaning he will give away more than 100% of current net worth by published plan). Scrooge Score of 58.3 from v2 pipeline is misleading because it uses a 10%-of-wealth giving-gap formula that doesn't account for the cumulative $60B already out-the-door exceeding current NW on a basis-fixed framing.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Use Buffett as the anchor/ceiling in lists and methodology pages: 'Buffett's $60B+ lifetime giving, with 99.5% of remainder committed at death, is the reference standard by which we judge other pledge signers.' Ellison gap to Buffett-equivalent = 99.8% short. A billionaire's position on the Scrooge list is effectively measured by distance from Buffett.

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.components_note: Computed in v2 pipeline using 40/20/20/20 weights. Do NOT publish. v2 score penalizes Buffett for giving only ~41% of net worth observable-to-date, but the methodology does not credit the $147B committed estate trust. If estate trust were counted, score would be <10.
scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: CRITICAL METHODOLOGY QUESTION for v3: does observable_giving / liquid_wealth use cumulative-out vs NW-at-signing (Forbes), or cumulative-out vs current NW (IPS)? The answer moves Buffett from 'fulfilled' to 'partially fulfilled'. Publish both framings and explain. Use Buffett as the training case that forces this decision.

right_of_reply.planned_outreach_route: Berkshire Hathaway Investor Relations (Marc Hamburg, CFO) + Debbie Bosanek (assistant to Buffett) — both have engaged media inquiries in the past (Bosanek gave on-record comment to Reuters for the Biden fact-check).
